2.title: Configuration | @db-ux/astro
2.content:
CONFIGURATION


ASTRO CONFIGURATION

Astro allows you to customize various aspects (including integrations) of your
project through the astro.config.mjs file located at the root of your project.
Instead of going through every possible configuration option, this guide focuses
on the key settings relevant to using @db-ux/astro. If you want to learn more
about Astro’s configuration options in general, please refer to the official
Astro documentation
[https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/configuration-reference/].


CONFIGURING @DB-UX/ASTRO

3.title: Getting Started | @db-ux/astro

4.title:
📄 UNDERSTAND FRONTMATTER ATTRIBUTES

Markdown documents include a header section providing document metadata known as
frontmatter. Markdown frontmatter uses yaml syntax. Each document needs to
include a frontmatter section. For a list of all required and supported
frontmatter attributes see ./template/types/global.d.ts
[./template/types/global.d.ts].

---
# This is the frontmatter section
layout: '@template/layouts/default' # Required
title: 'My page' # Required
description: 'This page is awesome!' # Required
toc: false # Disables the document's table of contents (optional)
nav: false # Disable the document's navigation sidebar (optional)
---
This is the document content.

5.title: Navigation | @db-ux/astro

6.title: Default Layout | @db-ux/astro
6.content:
DEFAULT LAYOUT


INTRODUCTION

The default layout is a versatile and responsive layout designed to cover most
common use cases (like documentation). It includes a header, footer and a main
content area. It will also render a table of contents if your page contains
headings. Most pages on this site use the default layout (including this one).


HOW TO USE

Markdown (.md) or MDX (.mdx):

---
layout: '@db-ux/astro/layouts/default'
---

Astro components (.astro):

---
import DefaultLayout from '@db-ux/astro/layouts/default';
---

<DefaultLayout>
  <!-- Your page content goes here -->
</DefaultLayout>


FRONT MATTER ATTRIBUTES

---
title: string # The title of the page, displayed in the browser, as the first level heading and used for SEO.
description: string # A brief description of the page, used for SEO.
toc: boolean # Whether to display a table of contents for the page. Default is true.
---

orderedDocs:
📄 UNDERSTAND FRONTMATTER ATTRIBUTES
